title: Circular No. 01/2025/TT-BCT stipulating tariff quotas for the importation of salt and poultry eggs in 2025.
summary: regulations on tariff quotas for the importation of salt and poultry eggs in 2025.
search_text:
01/2025/TT-BCT
Circular No. 01/2025/TT-BCT stipulating tariff quotas for the importation of salt and poultry eggs in 2025.
Circular
MINISTRY OF INDUSTRY AND TRADE
15/01/2025
02/03/2025
Official Gazette Nos. 203 + 204 dated 2025-01-30
regulations on tariff quotas for the importation of salt and poultry eggs in 2025.
Nguyễn Sinh Nhật Tân
thue
LEGAL NORMATIVE DOCUMENT

MINISTRY OF INDUSTRY AND TRADE

MINISTRY OF INDUSTRY AND TRADE

SOCIALIST REPUBLIC OF VIETNAM

Independence - Freedom - Happiness

No.: 01/2025/TT-BCT

Hanoi, January 15, 2025

CIRCULAR

Stipulations on tariff quotas for the importation of salt and poultry eggs in 2025

Pursuant to the Law on Foreign Trade Management dated June 12, 2017;

Pursuant to Decree No. 96/2022/ND-CP dated November 29, 2022 of the Government stipulating the functions, tasks, powers, and organizational structure of the Ministry of Industry and Trade; Decree No. 105/2024/ND-CP dated August 1, 2024 of the Government amending and supplementing several provisions of Decree No. 96/2022/ND-CP dated November 29, 2022 of the Government stipulating the functions, tasks, powers, and organizational structure of the Ministry of Industry and Trade and Decree No. 26/2018/ND-CP dated February 28, 2018 of the Government on the Charter of organization and operation of the Vietnam Electricity Group;

Pursuant to Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018 of the Government detailing several provisions of the Law on Foreign Trade Management;

Pursuant to Decree No. 26/2023/ND-CP dated May 31, 2023 of the Government on Export Tariffs, Preferential Import Tariffs, Lists of Goods and Absolute Tax Rates, Mixed Tax Rates, Import Duties outside Tariff Quotas; Decree No. 144/2024/ND-CP dated November 1, 2024 of the Government amending and supplementing several provisions of Decree No. 26/2023/ND-CP dated May 31, 2023 of the Government on Export Tariffs, Preferential Import Tariffs, Lists of Goods and Absolute Tax Rates, Mixed Tax Rates, Import Duties outside Tariff Quotas;

At the proposal of the Director of the Department of Import and Export;

The Minister of Industry and Trade issues a Circular stipulating tariff quotas for the importation of salt and poultry eggs in 2025.

Article 1. Quantity of tariff quotas for importing salt and poultry eggs in 2025

Table

Goods code

Goods name

Unit

Quantity

Notes

1

0407.21.00 and 0407.90.10

Chicken eggs

Dozen

72,104

Non-embryo commercial eggs

0407.29.10 and 0407.90.20

Duck, goose eggs

0407.29.90 and 0407.90.90

Other types

2

2501

Salt (including edible salt and denatured salt) and pure sodium chloride, whether in aqueous solution or containing added anti-caking agents or flow-enhancing agents; sea water.

Tons

92,400

Article 2. Method of allocating tariff quotas for importing salt and poultry eggs in 2025

Tariff quotas for importing salt and poultry eggs in 2025 are allocated according to the method stipulated in Article 9 of Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018 of the Government detailing several provisions of the Law on Foreign Trade Management and Article 15 of Circular No. 12/2018/TT-BCT dated June 15, 2018 of the Minister of Industry and Trade detailing several provisions of the Law on Foreign Trade Management and Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018 of the Government detailing several provisions of the Law on Foreign Trade Management.

Article 3. Subjects of allocation for tariff quotas for importing salt and poultry eggs in 2025

Tariff quotas for importing salt are allocated to traders who directly use it as raw materials for the production of medicine, medical products, and chemicals.

Tariff quotas for importing poultry eggs are allocated to traders with import needs.

Article 4. Commencement of allocation for tariff quotas for importing salt and poultry eggs in 2025

1. The commencement of allocation for tariff quotas for importing salt as raw materials for the production of medicine and medical products in 2025 is from the effective date of this Circular.

2. The Ministry of Industry and Trade consults with the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development to determine the commencement of allocation for tariff quotas for importing salt as raw materials for chemical production and tariff quotas for importing poultry eggs in 2025.

Article 5. Effective date

This Circular becomes effective from March 2, 2025, to December 31, 2025./.

KT. MINISTER

DEPUTY MINISTER

Nguyễn Sinh Nhật Tân

paragraphs: Regulations on tariff quotas for the importation of salt and poultry eggs in 2025
Pursuant to the Law on Foreign Trade Management dated June 12, 2017;

Pursuant to Decree No. 96/2022/ND-CP dated November 29, 2022 of the Government stipulating the functions, tasks, powers, and organizational structure of the Ministry of Industry and Trade; Decree No. 105/2024/ND-CP dated August 1, 2024 of the Government amending and supplementing several provisions of Decree No. 96/2022/ND-CP dated November 29, 2022 of the Government stipulating the functions, tasks, powers, and organizational structure of the Ministry of Industry and Trade and Decree No. 26/2018/ND-CP dated February 28, 2018 of the Government on the Charter of organization and operation of the Vietnam Electricity Group;
Pursuant to Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018 of the Government detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management;

Pursuant to Decree No. 26/2023/ND-CP dated May 31, 2023 of the Government on Export Tariffs, Preferential Import Tariffs, Lists of Goods and Absolute Tax Rates, Mixed Tax Rates, Import Duties outside Tariff Quotas; Decree No. 144/2024/ND-CP dated November 1, 2024 of the Government amending and supplementing several provisions of Decree No. 26/2023/ND-CP dated May 31, 2023 of the Government on Export Tariffs, Preferential Import Tariffs, Lists of Goods and Absolute Tax Rates, Mixed Tax Rates, Import Duties outside Tariff Quotas;
At the proposal of the Director of the Department of Import and Export;
The Minister of Industry and Trade issues a Circular regulating the import tariff quota for salt and poultry eggs in 2025.
Article 1. Import tariff quota amount for salt and poultry eggs in 2025
Salt (including table salt and denatured salt) and pure sodium chloride, whether or not in aqueous solution or containing anti-caking agents or flow improvers; sea water.
Article 2. Method for allocation of import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025

The import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025 are allocated according to the method stipulated in Article 9 of Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018, of the Government detailing certain provisions of the Law on Foreign Trade Management and Article 15 of Circular No. 12/2018/TT-BCT dated June 15, 2018, of the Minister of Industry and Trade detailing certain provisions of the Law on Foreign Trade Management and Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018, of the Government detailing certain provisions of the Law on Foreign Trade Management.
Article 3. Subjects for allocation of import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025
The import tariff quotas for salt are allocated to traders directly using it as a raw material for the production of medicine, medical products, and as a raw material for the production of chemicals.
The import tariff quotas for poultry eggs are allocated to traders who have import needs.
Article 4. Starting time for allocation of import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025

1. The starting point for the allocation of import tariff quotas for salt products as raw materials for the production of medicine and medical products in 2025 is from when this Circular comes into effect.

2. The Ministry of Industry and Trade is coordinating with the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development to determine the starting point for the allocation of import tariff quotas for salt products as raw materials for chemical production and import tariff quotas for poultry eggs in 2025.
This Circular takes effect from March 2, 2025, until the end of December 31, 2025./.

CIRCULAR

Regulations on import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025

Based on the Law on Foreign Trade Management dated June 12, 2017;

Based on Decree No. 96/2022/ND-CP dated November 29, 2022, of the Government stipulating the functions, duties, powers, and organizational structure of the Ministry of Industry and Trade; Decree No. 105/2024/ND-CP dated August 1, 2024, of the Government amending and supplementing a number of articles of Decree No. 96/2022/ND-CP dated November 29, 2022, of the Government stipulating the functions, duties, powers, and organizational structure of the Ministry of Industry and Trade and Decree No. 26/2018/ND-CP dated February 28, 2018, of the Government on the Charter of organization and operation of Vietnam Electricity Group;

Based on Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018, of the Government detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management;

Based on Decree No. 26/2023/ND-CP dated May 31, 2023, of the Government on Export Tariff, Preferential Import Tariff, List of Goods and Absolute Tax Rate, Mixed Tax, Import Tax Outside Tariff Quotas; Decree No. 144/2024/ND-CP dated November 1, 2024, of the Government amending and supplementing a number of articles of Decree No. 26/2023/ND-CP dated May 31, 2023, of the Government on Export Tariff, Preferential Import Tariff, List of Goods and Absolute Tax Rate, Mixed Tax, Import Tax Outside Tariff Quotas;

At the proposal of the Director of the Import and Export Department;

The Minister of Industry and Trade issues a Circular on import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025.

Article 1. Quantity of import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025

Salt (including edible salt and denatured salt) and pure sodium chloride, whether or not in aqueous solution or containing anti-caking agents or flow-enhancing agents; sea water.

Article 2. Method of allocation of import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025

Import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025 are allocated according to the method specified in Article 9 of Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018, of the Government detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management and Article 15 of Circular No. 12/2018/TT-BCT dated June 15, 2018, of the Minister of Industry and Trade detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management and Decree No. 69/2018/ND-CP dated May 15, 2018, of the Government detailing a number of articles of the Law on Foreign Trade Management.

Article 3. Subjects of allocation of import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025

Import tariff quotas for salt are allocated to traders directly using them as raw materials for the production of medicine, medical products, and as raw materials for chemical production.

Import tariff quotas for poultry eggs are allocated to traders with import needs.

Article 4. Starting point for allocation of import tariff quotas for salt and poultry eggs in 2025

1. The starting point for the allocation of import tariff quotas for salt products as raw materials for the production of medicine and medical products in 2025 is from when this Circular comes into effect.

2. The Ministry of Industry and Trade is coordinating with the Ministry of Agriculture and Rural Development to determine the starting point for the allocation of import tariff quotas for salt products as raw materials for chemical production and import tariff quotas for poultry eggs in 2025.

Article 5. Effective Date

This Circular takes effect from March 2, 2025, until the end of December 31, 2025./.

KT. MINISTER

DEPUTY MINISTER

Nguyen Sinh Nhat Tan
